reviews.review_msg: Jason is a great professor. He is passionate about what he teaches which makes learning a lot more enjoyable. His experience in the DRC made this class on social movements in Africa more detailed as he could talk about real experiences and invite guest lecturers who were part of ongoing social movements in Africa. I would highly recommend it.

Jason is a really caring prof, very approachable and overall a nice guy. But do not take this class unless you are interested in this stuff. It has a lot to do with war and conflicts. A lot of hard readings. Not hard to get a good grade because he makes sure the average is always a B+ but for me it was extremely boring and made no sense.

This course is interesting and engaging because the theories and required readings are applicable to situations in the real world, overall a solid course as an introduction to International Security. Jason is an exceptional prof, his interest in the subject shows, and he encourages students to ask questions/ask for clarification if needed.
He is actually understanding and somehow his course design is fit for studing online. Exceptional prof, trust me you would want to do another course with him.
Friendly prof and solid class overall, a good intro to international security and conflict

Jason has an extensive background within the field of international relations and his experience comes from work that he did in the Congo. He is extremely knowledgeable and it shows. His course conduct was excellent. Given that we were in the middle of a pandemic he altered the class structure so as to not place extra burden on us. Very caring.
